steps.title: Read the intersection point from the graph
steps.content: The graphed system's solution is the point where the two lines cross. Tracing both lines, they intersect at $$\left(\frac{3}{2}, 0\right)$$, meaning $$x=\frac{3}{2}$$ and $$y=0$$.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y=0$ and $x=\frac{3}{2}$': The solution to this system is $$\left(\frac{3}{2},0\right)$$ — matches the graph. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y=\frac{3}{2}$ and $x=0$': Solution would be $$\left(0,\frac{3}{2}\right)$$ — coordinates are swapped. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y=0$ and $x=1$': Solution would be $$(1,0)$$ — wrong x-value. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y=1$ and $x=0$': Solution would be $$(0,1)$$ — wrong values for both coordinates. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
